name: python-typing-patterns
description: Python type hints and type safety patterns. Triggers on: type hints, typing, TypeVar, Generic, Protocol, mypy, pyright, type annotation, overload, TypedDict.
capabilities: Shows Python type annotation syntax for variables, functions, and classes
Explains TypeVar, Generic, Protocol, and ParamSpec for flexible types
Demonstrates TypedDict for type-safe dictionary access
Covers mypy and pyright configuration for strict type checking
Provides runtime validation patterns using Pydantic, typeguard, and beartype
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This is a documentation/reference skill containing only Python typing patterns. All 293 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified markdown code formatting (backticks) as shell execution, TOML config keys as cryptographic algorithms, and Python typing library names as sensitive keywords. No actual network calls, credential handling, or command execution exists.
valueStatement: Python type hints improve code quality and catch bugs early. This skill provides patterns for TypeVar, Generic, Protocol, TypedDict, and runtime validation with Pydantic and typeguard.
limitations: Does not include type checker installation or execution
Does not modify user source code automatically
Does not integrate with IDEs or build systems directly
Requires Python 3.10+ for modern union syntax

useCases.title: Add type hints to code
useCases.description: Learn correct syntax for type annotations in Python 3.10+ including generics and protocols.

useCases.title: Design type-safe APIs
useCases.description: Create flexible APIs using TypeVar and Protocol for structural typing without inheritance.

useCases.title: Validate API data
useCases.description: Use Pydantic and typeguard to enforce types at runtime for API requests and config.

promptTemplates.title: Basic typing syntax
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to write type hints for a function that takes a list of strings and returns an integer.

promptTemplates.title: Generic functions
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use TypeVar to create a function that works with any type but preserves the type information?

promptTemplates.title: Protocol pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how Protocol works in Python and show an example where an object without inheritance satisfies an interface.

promptTemplates.title: Runtime validation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I validate function arguments at runtime using Pydantic v2 with field constraints?

bestPractices: Use Python 3.10+ union syntax (X | Y) instead of Optional[X] for clarity
Prefer Protocol over ABC for structural typing to allow duck typing
Enable strict mode in mypy or pyright to catch issues early
antiPatterns: Using Any extensively defeats the purpose of type checking
Avoid quotes around forward references (use from __future__ import annotations)
Do not mix Optional[X] with X | None inconsistently in the same codebase

faq.question: What Python version do I need?
faq.answer: Python 3.10+ for union syntax. Python 3.11+ for Self and TypeVarTuple.

faq.question: mypy vs pyright?
faq.answer: mypy is slower but mature. pyright is faster with better IDE integration.

faq.question: Type hints slow?
faq.answer: No, type hints are stripped at runtime. Runtime validation adds overhead.

faq.question: TypedDict vs dict?
faq.answer: TypedDict validates keys exist. Regular dict allows any keys with any types.

faq.question: Protocol inheritance?
faq.answer: Protocol uses structural typing. No explicit inheritance needed.

faq.question: Required vs NotRequired?
faq.answer: Required keys must exist. NotRequired keys are optional in TypedDict.
